header.title: iKomek 109 web platform for citizens, operators, and city administrators
header.summary: This web experience extends the same iKomek platform as mobile, using the same backend, entities, media, requests, statuses, saved locations, chats, and news so every action stays synchronized across channels.

sharedSystem.title: One backend, one data model
sharedSystem.description: The website is not a separate product. It is a production-ready desktop extension of the existing iKomek ecosystem and must use the same shared database and API contracts as the mobile application.
sharedSystem.sidebarNote: Any request, chat message, status update, profile edit, news item, or address change must instantly remain consistent between web and mobile.

home.title: Public landing experience with trust, urgency, and fast access
home.description: The homepage explains what iKomek is, how residents can report issues, where to see city news, and how to explore the live urban situation through map and analytics modules.
home.heroTitle: A government-grade civic-tech interface aligned with the mobile concept
quickAccessItems: Hero with report problem CTA and trust statement
Service category shortcuts from the shared taxonomy
Urgent city announcements with priority styling
Latest news feed with category tags and time relevance
Map snapshot showing Astana complaint activity
How it works steps tied to the shared request lifecycle

stats.label: City contact identity
stats.detail: Keep the existing brand anchor visible at all major trust points.

stats.label: Platform languages
stats.detail: Russian, Kazakh, and English are available from the same UI shell.

stats.label: Shared data source
stats.detail: Web and mobile operate on one backend and one canonical entity set.

sections.title: Official but warm
sections.description: Use white surfaces, orange actions, soft shadows, and high readability so the service feels reliable instead of bureaucratic.

sections.title: Cross-platform familiar
sections.description: The web version extends the same request logic, statuses, maps, and content structure already defined for mobile.

sections.title: Scalable entry point
sections.description: Public access, citizen self-service, operational tools, and administration all live inside one coherent information architecture.

auth.title: Shared sign-in flows with polished web transitions
auth.description: Authentication must respect the current backend methods and session rules while improving clarity through larger-screen layouts, clear validation, and helpful error recovery.
methods: Email login and registration with inline validation
Password recovery using existing backend recovery flow
Google and Apple entry points if already supported in backend
states: Animated step transitions between login, registration, and recovery
Localized field-level validation and error messages
Subtle success state after account creation or password reset
Session persistence across desktop browser refreshes

citizenDashboard.title: A command center for residents
citizenDashboard.description: The citizen dashboard summarizes the resident’s active relationship with the city and gives immediate access to requests, alerts, map exploration, and saved places.

panels.title: Request status overview
panels.description: Top summary cards show active, pending, and recently closed requests from the same shared request records used in mobile.

panels.title: Saved addresses
panels.description: Home, work, study, road, environment, and custom places appear as reusable quick-start sources for new issue reports.

panels.title: City pulse
panels.description: Latest alerts and news appear beside a quick map shortcut so the citizen dashboard feels informative, not only transactional.

requestFlow.title: Desktop-friendly version of the existing mobile request creation logic
requestFlow.description: The request form follows the same backend-driven taxonomy and submission rules as mobile, while using a spacious side-by-side layout for review, location confirmation, and media upload.

steps.title: Select address
steps.description: Choose from saved locations, manual search, map pick, or browser geolocation.

steps.title: Select place and issue
steps.description: Place variants and issue types are driven by shared category and reason entities.

steps.title: Describe and attach
steps.description: Add structured reason, free-text description, and photos through the shared attachment pipeline.

steps.title: Review and submit
steps.description: Show selected address, reason, description, map preview, and files before final confirmation.

inputs.title: Saved location logic
inputs.description: Address chips show type labels like Home, Work, Study, Road, Environment, and Other without deviating from the existing SavedLocation entity.

inputs.title: Reason structure
inputs.description: Reasons are not hardcoded per web page. They come from the shared RequestReason dataset already serving mobile flows.

inputs.title: Fast completion UX
inputs.description: Use sticky summaries, drag-and-drop uploads, address prediction, and contextual hints to reduce form fatigue on desktop.

requestList.title: Citizen request management with cards and table modes
requestList.description: Residents need a clear history of all requests with meaningful filters and a visual understanding of where each issue stands today.
features: Toggle between card view and compact table view
Filter by status, category, address, date, urgency, and sort order
Expose the current stage through status color, progress line, and latest update snippet
Open the request detail page without losing the list filter context

requestDetails.title: A full record of each issue across mobile and web
requestDetails.description: The detail page is the same logical request object shown through a richer desktop layout with map, gallery, timeline, and messaging context visible together.

columns.title: Core details
items: Title, address, place, geo point, issue type, reason, description
Created date, current status, and assigned department when relevant

columns.title: Operational history
items: Status history timeline backed by RequestStatusHistory
Shared chat thread and notifications synchronized with mobile

chat.title: Request-specific communication synchronized across channels
chat.description: Chat is tied to the request entity so all citizens and operators see the same conversation and status context, regardless of whether they use web or mobile.

roles.title: Citizen actions
items: Attach extra photos when policy allows
Receive clarification requests and status notices

roles.title: Operator actions
items: Send progress updates and completion confirmations
Trigger status changes from the same operational workspace

news.title: Structured city information with clear urgency levels
news.description: The website exposes the same city communication stream as mobile, but gives more room for filtering, detailed reading, and location-aware alert presentation.

priorities.title: Emergency works and city-level incidents
priorities.description: Use high-contrast alert cards, persistent header visibility, and time/location scope for urgent utility, safety, and weather disruptions.

priorities.title: Planned disruption and cautionary updates
priorities.description: Highlight road closures, transport changes, and local service interruptions with map relevance and active periods.

priorities.title: News, public service updates, and civic context
priorities.description: Show the broader city narrative through categorized editorial cards and deeper article pages linked from the dashboard and home.

map.title: OpenLayers-based city visibility for public issues and personal requests
map.description: The map must start in Astana and clearly separate personal activity from the public issue landscape through layers, style systems, clustering, and focus modes rather than simple text labels.
behaviors: Default city extent centered on Astana with district-aware overlays
Distinct marker system for personal requests versus public complaints
Layer toggles for all requests, my requests, heatmap, categories, status, and date ranges
Clustering at low zoom, discrete points at higher zoom, and muted public markers in personal focus mode
Marker click opens a side panel preview and can transition into full request detail
Map and list views stay synchronized through a shared map state store

analytics.title: Citizen-friendly analytics
analytics.description: Summaries include total requests, active issues, average response time, top categories, and district comparisons in plain language.

analytics.title: Operational insight
analytics.description: Operators and admins can switch to denser analytical panels showing urgency spikes, complaint density zones, and backlog concentration.

analytics.title: Future-ready map engine
analytics.description: Keep map rendering isolated so clustering, heatmaps, district choropleths, and live event layers can evolve without touching feature screens.

profile.title: Personal data management linked to the same user account as mobile
profile.description: Citizens manage personal details, language, saved addresses, notification preferences, and security settings in one settings area connected to the shared user and location entities.

cards.title: Saved addresses
cards.description: Each saved place stores label, category, address metadata, and geo coordinates using the existing SavedLocation model.

cards.title: Profile and security
cards.description: Personal information, linked auth methods, password controls, and biometric references remain consistent with the underlying account.

cards.title: Localization and notifications
cards.description: Language choice, alert preferences, and visual density settings support three languages and future accessibility enhancements.

operator.title: High-density operational workspace for real-time triage
operator.description: The operator interface is optimized for sustained daily handling of large request volumes with fast filtering, detailed inspection, and coordinated assignment workflows.

panels.title: Live queue
panels.description: Real-time request stream with priority cues, district grouping, and quick actions for opening details, updating status, and replying.

panels.title: Smart filtering
panels.description: Filter by category, status, district, urgency, date, address, and citizen while preserving keyboard-driven speed.

panels.title: Execution controls
panels.description: Assign requests to departments, attach internal notes, review media, moderate content, and upload proof of completion.

workflow: Prioritize by urgency, unresolved duration, and district spikes
Review full request context, photos, previous status history, and chat
Respond to citizen, assign department, and log internal operational notes
Track execution proof, validate completion, and close the issue with synchronized status updates

admin.title: City-level oversight, configuration, and analytics control
admin.description: Admins need one web environment for user management, taxonomies, communications, geographic analytics, and long-term service performance monitoring.

modules.title: System governance
modules.description: Manage users, roles, operators, departments, and access policies from the same shared role model used across the platform.

modules.title: Taxonomy and content
modules.description: Maintain categories, predefined reasons, news, alerts, and platform configuration without duplicating structures for web.

modules.title: Analytics and trends
modules.description: Monitor district patterns, request growth, resolution times, service quality, and geographic load through rich dashboards.

admin.executorDescription: Department users receive assigned requests, update progress, upload completion evidence, and close work orders through the same shared request lifecycle.
admin.futureDescription: This architecture supports modular expansion into SLA dashboards, AI-assisted categorization, district scorecards, and dedicated departmental workspaces without splitting the data model.

saved.title: Saved places
saved.empty: Saved addresses will appear here after you add them.

architecture.title: Separation of UI, domain, map logic, and backend integration
architecture.description: The frontend is organized by feature boundaries and shared platform layers so it can scale from a public website into a full city operations interface without becoming brittle.
decisions: Feature modules own screen composition, while reusable UI primitives remain small and portable.
Localization lives in dedicated language files instead of hardcoded UI strings.
API access is abstracted behind services so components never call backend endpoints directly.
Map behavior is isolated and should evolve into a dedicated OpenLayers engine module.
Stores mirror shared backend entities rather than inventing web-only data structures.
The same requests, chat history, news, statuses, media, saved locations, and geo data must render consistently on both mobile and web.
